title: Past Events
subtitle: Previous editions of the Machine Learning Workshop

workshopHistory.title: ML Workshop 2024
workshopHistory.description: The 2024 edition focused on generative models and their applications in computer vision, featuring keynotes from leading researchers in the field.

workshopHistory.title: ML Workshop 2023
workshopHistory.description: The 2023 workshop explored self-supervised learning approaches and their impact on computer vision tasks, with a special focus on few-shot learning.

workshopHistory.title: ML Workshop 2022
workshopHistory.description: The inaugural workshop in 2022 brought together researchers to discuss foundational machine learning techniques for computer vision applications.

pastPapers.title: Diffusion Models for High-Resolution Image Generation
pastPapers.authors: A. Smith, B. Johnson, C. Williams

pastPapers.title: Transformer Architectures for Multi-Modal Learning
pastPapers.authors: D. Brown, E. Davis, F. Wilson

pastPapers.title: Self-Supervised Learning for Visual Representation
pastPapers.authors: G. Martinez, H. Lee, I. Kim

pastPapers.title: Few-Shot Learning with Meta-Learning Approaches
pastPapers.authors: J. Chen, K. Wang, L. Zhang

pastPapers.title: Efficient Neural Networks for Mobile Devices
pastPapers.authors: M. Patel, N. Singh, O. Thompson

pastKeynotes.title: Advances in Generative Models
pastKeynotes.speaker: Prof. Alice Johnson, 2024
pastKeynotes.description: This keynote explored the latest advances in generative models, focusing on diffusion models and their applications in image and video generation.

pastKeynotes.title: Self-Supervised Learning Revolution
pastKeynotes.speaker: Dr. Robert Smith, 2023
pastKeynotes.description: This keynote discussed how self-supervised learning approaches have revolutionized computer vision tasks, reducing the need for large labeled datasets.

resources.title: Workshop Datasets
resources.description: A collection of benchmark datasets used in previous workshop challenges, available for download and research purposes.

resources.title: Code Repositories
resources.description: GitHub repositories containing code implementations of selected papers from previous workshops.

resources.title: Presentation Slides
resources.description: A collection of presentation slides from keynote speakers and selected paper presentations at previous workshops.
